title: Foundation
description: Initial release establishing the core 8-section framework. Covers premise, layers, roles, rituals, artifacts, delivery cycle, communication patterns, and operating principles.
highlights: 8-section governance framework
4-layer model: Strategic, Product, Execution, Dependencies
10 operating axioms
Mermaid flow diagrams
Hello World cycle walkthrough
Escape Hatch / Intent Correction pattern

title: Human Craft Layer
description: Redefines how developers and QA practitioners contribute in an agent-driven team. Introduces two new human roles that evolve — rather than replace — traditional craft.
highlights: Craft Engineer role: governs how agents write code
Quality Advocate role: defines what good looks like
Craft Review ritual: human judgment pre-flag
Axiom A11: Agents amplify human craft, not replace it

title: Consistency & Governance
description: Fixes naming inconsistencies, closes governance gaps in memory and gate authority, adds Escape Hatch pattern, and clarifies human decision boundaries throughout.
highlights: Fixed Planner/Orchestrator naming inconsistency across all sections
Added Escape Hatch (intent correction) pattern to delivery cycle
Added A12: Agents write memory, humans govern it
Clarified Quality Advocate authority vs PO final gate call
Added PO backup/delegate guidance to prevent indefinite gate holds
Craft Review now visible in delivery cycle diagram
Drift check cadence aligned between Section 02 and Section 04

title: Cycle Flow Visual
description: Restores the Mermaid cycle flow diagram from v1, updated to include Craft Review, Intent Correction, and production fail paths introduced in v2/v3.
highlights: Mermaid cycle flow diagram restored and updated for v4 role model
Craft Review step added to cycle flow (Craft Eng + QA before PO gate)
Intent Correction and production fail paths shown in visual flow
Last remaining 'Planner' reference fixed in Section 07 comm-card

title: Authority vs Knowledge
description: Corrects Principle 02 to distinguish layered decision authority from shared knowledge — agents already cross layers freely; what the framework enforces is accountability, not information siloing.
highlights: Principle 02 reframed: 'Authority is layered, knowledge is shared'
Section 02 intro: 'distinct knowledge' → 'distinct decision authority'

title: Didactic Layer
description: Adds teaching scaffolding for new adopters and trims verbose role descriptions, making the framework easier to learn without reducing its substance.
highlights: Reading guide callout: learning path (01→03→06→08) and reference sections
Before-your-first-cycle checklist: 4 required artifacts named explicitly
Role transition table: current role → IDF role in 5 rows
Intent good/bad example added to PO card
Craft Engineer and Quality Advocate paragraphs trimmed to 2 sentences
Section 03 intro: 'knowledge' → 'decision authority' (consistency with v4.1)
Last Planner reference removed from Dependency Broker body text

title: Measurement System
description: Adds Section 09 with a minimum metric system: DORA four metrics reframed for IDF's flag-gated cycle model plus three IDF-native signals, all sourced from existing artifacts.
highlights: New Section 09 — Measurement with 7 defined metrics
DORA four reframed: Cycle throughput, Intent-to-flag time, Flag-OFF response, Escape rate
IDF-native signals: Gate first-pass rate, Intent clarity rate, PO review time
Explicit anti-metrics: story points, velocity, burndown excluded by name
All metrics sourced from existing artifacts — no new tooling required
A13 added: Measure outcomes, not activity

title: Narrative Reflow
description: Reorders all sections to match a natural learning arc — principles before mechanics, cycle before structure — without changing any framework content.
highlights: Section order redesigned: Why → Principles → Roles → Cycle → Structure → Rituals → Artifacts → Communication → Measurement
Axioms promoted to Section 02 — philosophical foundation before mechanics
Delivery Cycle promoted to Section 04 — see it in action before the supporting system
Axioms section gains framing intro: 'Before the mechanics, the invariants'
Structure section intro updated to acknowledge the reader has already seen the cycle
Reading guide updated: Sections 01–04 are foundation, 05–09 are reference

title: Flags as Context
description: Formalizes feature flags as agent context boundaries: flags-ON code plus current intent is the complete context agents should reason about; dead flags are context pollution.
highlights: A14: Flags-ON code plus current intent is the complete agent context
Feature flag registry: three lifecycle states (Pending-OFF, Live-ON, Dead-OFF)
Feature flag registry: dual purpose — deployment safety + agent context hygiene
Context Reset ritual: dead flag audit added to required output
Tech Lead gains explicit ownership of dead flag cleanup

title: Governance & Craft
description: Promotes the v4.x series to a stable major release: corrected principles, teaching scaffolding, a full measurement system, redesigned narrative arc, and flags as agent context boundaries.
highlights: Principle 02 corrected: authority is layered, knowledge is shared
Teaching layer: reading guide, role transition table, intent good/bad example
Section 09 — Measurement: DORA four reframed for IDF + three IDF-native signals
Narrative reflow: sections ordered as a learning arc (01–04 foundation, 05–09 reference)
A14: Flags-ON code plus current intent is the complete agent context
Feature flag lifecycle: Pending-OFF / Live-ON / Dead-OFF with context governance
14 operating axioms total

title: Technology-Agnostic
description: Rewrites IDF as a universal AI methodology: abstracts all tool-specific artifacts, defines roles by decision authority, renames Rituals to Signal Events, and adds a Context Debt Management section.
highlights: Artifact names abstracted: CLAUDE.md → System Memory, .claude/skills/ → Capability Library, .flags.json → Feature Governance Registry
Roles redefined by decision authority: Orchestrator as Intent Translator/Decomposer, Builder as Execution Entity, Guardian as Automated Quality/Policy Governor
Section 06 renamed Rituals → Signal Events; Intent Injection and Context Reset formalized as named events
New Section 10: Context Debt Management — defines context pollution types and mandates audit checklist
Framework no longer assumes any specific AI toolchain — works with Claude, GPT-4, local LLMs, or custom agent swarms

title: The Manifesto
description: Adds Section 00 — a four-value manifesto at the foundation of IDF, connecting its mechanics to Agile values and making explicit what the framework prioritizes above all process.
highlights: New Section 00 — Manifesto: four values in the style of the Agile Manifesto
Human judgment over automated execution
Functioning systems and clean context over comprehensive documentation
User outcomes over process compliance
Responding to better information over following the original plan
Classic Agile caveat preserved; cover origin updated to reference Agile values lineage

title: Manifesto Voice
description: Rewrites the four manifesto card descriptions to match IDF's authoritative voice; fixes manifesto-right color from near-invisible to readable.
highlights: Manifesto card body text rewritten in IDF's precise, declarative style
Removed casual language: colloquial time references, first-person plural, emotional framing
manifesto-right color changed from var(--border2) to var(--text3) — readable but clearly secondary

title: Values Refinement
description: Renames Section 00 to Core Values, removes emojis and the Agile caveat, and rewrites the opening paragraph as a precise professional statement.
highlights: Section 00 renamed from Manifesto to Core Values
h2 updated: 'What the framework prioritizes — and why it matters'
Opening paragraph rewritten: explicit value priorities, not aspirational statements
Emojis removed from all four value cards
Agile Manifesto caveat removed

title: Redundancy Pass
description: Removes repeated concepts across sections — same ideas stated once, in the right place, at the right depth.
highlights: "Agents execute, humans govern" cut from Section 01 intro (canonical home: cover tagline + Principle 03)
Context Debt framing cut from Section 00 Value 2 and Section 07 Capability Library (canonical home: Section 10)
"Every deploy behind a flag" prose cut from Section 07 registry card (canonical home: Axiom A04)
"Dead-OFF flags must be removed" paragraph cut from Section 07 (canonical home: A14 + Section 10 checklist)
Orchestrator escalation-on-ambiguity explanation cut from Section 08 comm card (canonical home: A03 + Section 03 role card)

title: Simplification
description: Strips sections 00-02 to essentials, removes theory and duplicate principles, and adds narrative connectors across all ten sections.
highlights: Section 00: card body paragraphs removed — X/over/Y statements stand alone
Section 01 renamed Getting Started: theory cut, 6 duplicate principles removed, 3-sentence practical intro added
Section 02: axioms reduced from 14 to 12 — A11 (duplicate value) removed, A05+A12 merged into single memory governance rule, A14 trimmed
Sections 03–10: single bridging sentence added to each intro — the framework now reads as one connected document

title: Values Merge
description: Merges Section 00 (Core Values) into Section 01 (Getting Started) and sharpens all four value labels for precision.
highlights: Sections 00 and 01 merged into a single Getting Started section
Value grid now appears inline after the 3-sentence intro, above the callout boxes
"Functioning systems and clean context" → "Usability and clean context"
"User outcomes over process compliance" → "Outcomes over process"
"Responding to better information over following the original plan" → "Respond to reality over following a plan"

title: Cover Tagline
description: Replaces the verbose cover tagline with a single declarative statement that positions IDF against traditional agile.
highlights: Cover tagline replaced: "Traditional agile was built for human bandwidth. This isn't."

title: Quickstart
description: Restores the Quickstart section from v1 as Section 11, updated for current IDF terminology and the technology-agnostic artifact names.
highlights: New Section 11 — Quickstart: Your first intent-driven cycle
6-step walkthrough: Intent → Decompose → Build → Gate → Live-ON → Monitor
Artifact names updated: INTENT_LOG, FEATURE_GOVERNANCE_REGISTRY, CLIENT_SIGNALS, GATE_REPORT
Flag lifecycle updated: Pending-OFF → Live-ON (v6.0 terminology)
Orchestrator escalation behaviour and three-paragraph gate report rule reflected in step descriptions

title: Mobile-First
description: Adds full mobile responsiveness at 767px and below — all grids reflow, padding scales, layer table stacks — without changing the desktop layout.
highlights: Comprehensive @media (max-width: 767px) block added
Cover, section padding, parable, footer all scale to mobile
Layer table: label rotates horizontal and stacks above content on mobile
Rituals table: header hidden, rows stack with inline field labels
All CSS-class grids collapse to single column (manifesto, roles, artifacts, comm)
Inline style grids overridden via attribute selectors with !important
Nav bar tightened at 600px: badge hidden, pills smaller
Parable 'The Driver and the Pilot' added between cover and Section 01

title: Structure Clarity
description: Rewrites Section 05 layer table so every role is individually tagged as human or agent, and every signal event shows who triggers it and when.
highlights: Every role in all four layers now carries its own inline human/agent tag
Every signal event now shows a human or auto trigger badge plus who and when
Strategic: Strategy agent explicitly tagged as agent
Product: Orchestrator moved to agent; PO/UX explicitly tagged as human
Execution: Orchestrator, Builder, Guardian each tagged as agent individually
Dependencies: Dependency broker tagged as agent; Affected POs and Tech leads as human
Auto-triggered events (Cycle kick-off, Auto-test gate, Dependency sync, Blocker escalation) clearly distinguished from human-triggered events

title: Five Rules
description: Rewrites Section 02 from 12 role-tied axioms to 5 behavioral rules — each expressed as a system behavior, not a role obligation.
highlights: Section 02: 12 axioms → 5 rules (R1–R5)
R1: Express outcomes, not instructions (covers A01 + A02)
R2: Ambiguity stops execution, it never shapes it (covers A03)
R3: Every change ships behind a flag (covers A04 + A12)
R4: Written context is the only context (covers A05 + A06)
R5: Measure what reaches the client (covers A07–A11)
All role names removed from Section 02 — roles are introduced in Section 03

title: Section Heading
description: Replaces the Getting Started h2 with the cover tagline, making the positioning statement the entry point to the framework body.
highlights: Section 01 h2 changed: "IDF in two sentences." → "Traditional agile was built for human bandwidth. This isn't."
